groups.blurb: Everyday dev — commits, debugging, docs, ADRs, handoffs
Pull-request lifecycle — create, review, resolve, merge
Spec-driven pipeline — start, implement, verify, deliver
Security scanners — secrets, deps, threat models & more
Lightweight board tracker — create, move, list, configure

commands.name: commit
commands.description: Safe commit — inspects repo state, stages intentionally, screens for secrets (.env, keys, tokens), drafts a Conventional Commits message, and links the current board task.
phrases: marvin, stage and commit my changes
marvin, make a conventional commit linked to the board

commands.name: pr-create
commands.description: Open a pull request with a structured description and verification checklist; picks up board-task context when present.
phrases: marvin, create a PR for this branch
marvin, push and open a PR

commands.name: pr-review
commands.description: Review a pull request on GitHub and post the review there — inline comments by severity plus a summary.

commands.name: pr-resolve
commands.description: Resolve open PR review threads — fetch the unresolved ones, plan and apply fixes, push, then reply and mark each resolved.
phrases: marvin, apply the reviewer's suggestions on PR 42

commands.name: pr-merge
commands.blurb: Merge a PR, then sync the base
commands.description: Merge a pull request, then switch back to the base branch and pull.
phrases: marvin, merge PR 42 and sync the base

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Hypothesis-driven root-cause analysis: reproduce the bug, gather evidence, rank hypotheses, confirm the mechanism at file:line, then propose a minimal fix.
phrases: marvin, why is this test failing?
marvin, help me debug this crash
marvin, find the root cause of this error

commands.name: adr
commands.description: Draft an Architecture Decision Record capturing context, alternatives, the decision, and consequences. Lands as status proposed; ratification is the separate human-run adr-accept.
phrases: marvin, record this decision as an ADR
marvin, write an architecture decision record
marvin, capture the rationale for this choice

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Generate a changelog from git history between tags, dates, or arbitrary refs.
phrases: marvin, what changed since the last release?
marvin, draft release notes since v0.1.0

commands.name: readme
commands.description: Generate or refresh README.md from actual codebase analysis.
phrases: marvin, refresh the readme from the code

commands.name: migration-plan
commands.blurb: Plan a migration or major refactor
commands.description: Plan a migration or major refactor: dependency analysis, sequenced steps, risks, and a rollback strategy.
phrases: marvin, how do we refactor this safely?
marvin, draft a migration plan with a rollback

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain how code works — logic, architecture, and design rationale — without changing it.
phrases: marvin, walk me through this module
marvin, what does this function do?

commands.name: docs-search
commands.description: Search and synthesize project documentation — ADRs, README, runbooks, conventions.
phrases: marvin, find the docs on the verify gate
marvin, search the project docs for this
commands.example: /marvin:docs-search how does the verify gate work?

commands.name: handoff
commands.description: Capture the session's full context into a durable handoff document plus a paste-ready prompt to continue in a fresh session.
phrases: marvin, save the context so I can continue later
marvin, prep a handoff for a fresh session

commands.name: handoff-list
commands.description: List the saved session-continuation handoff documents under .marvin/handoff/, newest first.
phrases: marvin, show me the saved handoff docs
marvin, what handoffs do we have?

commands.name: lessons
commands.description: Team lessons-learned store — capture and recall bug-patterns and gotchas across tasks (.marvin/memory).
phrases: marvin, save this as a lesson
marvin, what lessons do we have on this?
marvin, recall past gotchas for this bug

commands.name: help
commands.description: This welcome dashboard and the full command index; pass a group to focus the reference.

commands.name: dashboard
commands.description: Whole-toolbox state report: board, config, git, artifact inventories, ADR corpus, and local usage.
phrases: marvin, what's the state of the toolbox?
marvin, give me the whole-project report

commands.name: reports
commands.description: Unified viewer over every generated .marvin/ report — security scans, refactor registers and plans, task specs, verification, handoffs — newest first, with per-report freshness.
phrases: marvin, what reports do we have?
marvin, open the latest security report

commands.name: adr-review
commands.description: Deep review of one proposed ADR — section validation, codebase grounding, formal auto-fixes, and a readiness verdict. Never sets accepted.
phrases: marvin, is ADR 31 ready to accept?

commands.name: adr-accept
commands.description: Ratify a proposed ADR — proposed → accepted with a date stamp, through the fail-closed readiness gate. Human-run.
phrases: marvin, mark the decision record accepted

commands.name: adr-audit
commands.description: Read-only lint of the whole ADR corpus — dangling references, numbering holes, broken supersede pairs, stale index.

commands.name: adr-coverage
commands.description: Gap analysis — recorded ADRs vs the decisions visible in the actual stack, ranked by blast radius.
phrases: marvin, which ADRs are we missing?

commands.name: adr-supersede
commands.description: Roll back an accepted ADR properly — a successor record supersedes it and the links pair both ways. Human-run.
phrases: marvin, replace an accepted decision record

commands.name: adr-sync
commands.blurb: Refresh the ADR digest in CLAUDE.md (human-run)
commands.description: Regenerate the Architecture-decisions digest in CLAUDE.md from accepted ADRs only. Human-run.
phrases: marvin, refresh the decisions in CLAUDE.md

commands.name: task-start
commands.description: Phase 1 of the task pipeline — a structured dialogue that produces an immutable, testable spec under .marvin/task/.
phrases: marvin, define the task for pagination

commands.name: task-implement
commands.description: Execute a ready spec interactively in the current session, then auto-chain into verify and deliver.
phrases: marvin, build out the ready spec

commands.name: task-verify
commands.description: Run the project quality gates — tests, lint, type-check, build — with automatic stack detection, and write verification.md.
phrases: marvin, are the tests and lint green?

commands.name: task-deliver
commands.description: Commit changes and open a pull request; refuses if verification failed.
phrases: marvin, commit and open a PR for this

commands.name: task-summary
commands.description: Summarise what a task delivered — acceptance criteria vs verification, commits, lessons, and links.
phrases: marvin, summarize what this task delivered
marvin, give me the delivery digest
marvin, recap the task's acceptance criteria

commands.name: sec-scan
commands.description: Comprehensive security audit aligned with OWASP Top 10:2025 — orchestrates secrets, dependency, and IaC scans plus deep static analysis.
phrases: marvin, do a full OWASP audit

commands.name: sec-secrets
commands.description: Deep scan for leaked secrets, credentials, and API keys across code, config, and git history.
phrases: marvin, check for exposed API keys
marvin, look for credentials in the git history

commands.name: sec-deps
commands.description: Audit dependencies for known vulnerabilities, license risk, and maintenance health.
phrases: marvin, check dependency CVEs and licenses

commands.name: sec-gate
commands.description: Fast security check on staged or recent changes — a lightweight pre-commit gate.
phrases: marvin, quick security check on my changes
marvin, run the pre-commit security gate
marvin, is this staged diff safe?

commands.name: sec-threat-model
commands.description: Generate a STRIDE-based threat model for a feature, system, or the whole application.
phrases: marvin, run a STRIDE analysis on the upload flow
marvin, what are the attack surfaces here?

commands.name: sec-iac
commands.description: Security review of Infrastructure-as-Code — Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes, Docker, Helm.
phrases: marvin, review the Terraform for security

commands.name: sec-ci
commands.description: Audit CI/CD pipelines for supply-chain risks, secret exposure, and excessive permissions.
phrases: marvin, check the GitHub Actions for supply-chain risk

commands.name: sec-fix
commands.description: Generate and verify a minimal, tested patch for a security finding, with a regression test.
phrases: marvin, patch CVE-2024-1234 with a test

commands.name: sec-compliance
commands.description: Check code against OWASP ASVS compliance requirements — a structured compliance matrix.
phrases: marvin, run a compliance gap analysis

commands.name: sec-pentest
commands.description: Generate a penetration-testing checklist tailored to the specific application.
phrases: marvin, give me a pentest checklist
marvin, plan a penetration test for this app
marvin, what should I test for exploits?

commands.name: sec-report
commands.description: List the structured security-audit reports under .marvin/security/ — typed findings by severity, newest first.
phrases: marvin, show me past audit findings
marvin, what security scans have we run?

commands.name: refactor-audit
commands.description: Whole-project structural refactoring audit — architecture map, churn×size hotspots, dependency tangles, dead-code candidates. Read-only.
phrases: marvin, where is the tech debt?

commands.name: refactor-smells
commands.description: Scoped code-smell scan of a path, module, or diff — smells, anti-patterns, and naming inconsistencies. Read-only.
phrases: marvin, scan this module for code smells
marvin, find the smells in this diff

commands.name: refactor-plan
commands.description: Turn selected refactoring findings into a sequenced, risk-annotated plan; oversized items route to the task pipeline.
phrases: marvin, sequence findings F3 and F4 into steps
marvin, turn these findings into a plan

commands.name: refactor-apply
commands.description: Execute exactly one behaviour-preserving refactoring step under hard rails — verify green before and after, rollback on red.
phrases: marvin, apply the next refactor step
marvin, execute step 2 of the plan
marvin, do the refactoring under the gates

commands.name: track-menu
commands.description: Open the board action menu.
phrases: marvin, what can I do on the board?

commands.name: track-new
commands.description: Create a board task — bug, feature, chore, or spike — through an interactive form.
phrases: marvin, add a bug to the board

commands.name: track-list
commands.description: List the board — the full status-grouped list, the current-branch + work-in-progress view, or the tracked tasks linking out to the external tracker.
phrases: marvin, what am I working on?

commands.name: track-show
commands.description: Show one board task in detail.
phrases: marvin, give me the details of task 12

commands.name: track-start
commands.blurb: Pick up a task, branch off
commands.description: Pick a todo task, create its branch, and mark it work-in-progress.
phrases: marvin, pick up the next todo
marvin, begin work on this task

commands.name: track-move
commands.description: Move a board task — to review, to done, or to any configured status.

commands.name: track-config
commands.description: Show or edit the board configuration (.marvin/config.json).
